framework_info.framework_name: NIST SP 800-53 Security and Privacy Controls
framework_info.framework_description: NIST Special Publication 800-53 provides a catalog of security and privacy controls for federal information systems and organizations to protect organizational operations and assets, individuals, other organizations, and the Nation from a diverse set of threats including hostile attacks, human errors, natural disasters, structural failures, foreign intelligence entities, and privacy risks.

policies.policy_title: NIST SP 800-53 Release 5.2.0 Security and Privacy Controls Amendment
policies.policy_description: Amendment detailing updates and new controls in NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5.2.0, focusing on software update security, resiliency, and related enhancements.
policies.scope: Federal information systems and organizations using NIST SP 800-53 controls
policies.objective: Integrate new and revised controls to address software development, resiliency, flaw remediation, and update management.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: SA-15(13) Development Process, Standards, and Tools | Logging Syntax
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: New control enhancement requiring clear logging syntax for development processes, standards, and tools.
subpolicies.control: New Control Enhancement to address more clear requirements for logging.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Explicitly identified as a new control enhancement in the table of changes.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: SA-15(13) Development Process, Standards, and Tools | Logging Syntax | New Control Enhancement to address more clear requirements for logging.

compliance_records.ComplianceTitle: SA-15(13) Development Process, Standards, and Tools | Logging Syntax
compliance_records.ComplianceItemDescription: New Control Enhancement to address more clear requirements for logging.
compliance_records.Scope: All development teams across the organization
compliance_records.Objective: Ensure consistent and auditable logging practices in development processes
compliance_records.PotentialRiskScenarios: Inconsistent log formats leading to missed incidents

risk_details.risk_summary: Potential non‑compliance with logging standards could increase operational risk.
risk_details.source_logic: Derived from control text and subpolicy description.

compliance_records.SubPolicyName: SA-15(13) Development Process, Standards, and Tools | Logging Syntax
compliance_records.PolicyTitle: NIST SP 800-53 Release 5.2.0 Security and Privacy Controls Amendment

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: New control addressing cyber resiliency by design in software development.
subpolicies.control: Added to address cyber resiliency by design.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Listed as a new control not present in SP 800-53B baseline.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: SA-24, Design for Cyber Resiliency | New in final based on stakeholder feedback. Added to address cyber resiliency by design.

compliance_records.ComplianceItemDescription: Added to address cyber resiliency by design.
compliance_records.Objective: Integrate cyber resiliency considerations into design and architecture
compliance_records.BusinessUnitsCovered: All engineering and product development units
compliance_records.PotentialRiskScenarios: Insufficient cyber resiliency leading to system compromise
compliance_records.RiskBusinessImpact: Service disruption, data loss, financial loss

risk_details.risk_summary: Potential breach of resilience requirements could expose the organization to operational and financial harm.
risk_details.source_logic: Assessment of design artifacts and implementation against resilience standards.
risk_details.evidence_source: Design documentation, code reviews, and testing reports.

compliance_records.PolicyTitle: NIST SP 800-53 Release 5.2.0 Security and Privacy Controls Amendment

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: SI-02(07) Flaw Remediation | Root Cause Analysis
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Control enhancement requiring root cause analysis for flaw remediation.
subpolicies.control: New control enhancement to address conducting a root cause analysis.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Identified as a new control enhancement addressing a gap from NIST SSDF.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: SI-02(07) Flaw Remediation | Root Cause Analysis | New control enhancement to address conducting a root cause analysis.

compliance_records.ComplianceTitle: Root Cause Analysis for Flaw Remediation
compliance_records.ComplianceItemDescription: Perform root cause analysis for all flaw remediation activities to prevent recurrence.
compliance_records.Objective: Ensure systematic identification of underlying causes of defects
compliance_records.PotentialRiskScenarios: Inadequate analysis leading to repeated defects and extended downtime
compliance_records.RiskBusinessImpact: Increased operational costs and delayed releases

risk_details.risk_summary: Root cause analysis not consistently applied results in unresolved defect patterns.
risk_details.source_logic: Analysis of defect recurrence rates and post‑remediation audit findings.
risk_details.evidence_source: Defect tracking system reports and audit logs.

compliance_records.SubPolicyName: SI-02(07) Flaw Remediation | Root Cause Analysis
compliance_records.PolicyTitle: NIST SP 800-53 Release 5.2.0 Security and Privacy Controls Amendment

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Revision expanding the scope from user software to all organization-defined software.
subpolicies.control: Revised existing control enhancement to address all organization-defined software instead of only organization-defined user software.

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: Explicitly described as a revision to existing control requirement.
ai_analysis.source_excerpt: SI-07(12) Revised existing control enhancement to address all organization-defined software instead of only organization-defined user software.

compliance_records.ComplianceItemDescription: Expanded scope from organization-defined user software to all organization-defined software.
compliance_records.Objective: Ensure comprehensive coverage of software assets in governance processes.
compliance_records.PotentialRiskScenarios: Uncontrolled software proliferation leading to compliance gaps.
compliance_records.RiskBusinessImpact: Potential non‑compliance with software governance policies.
compliance_records.PossibleDamage: Operational inefficiencies and increased audit findings.

risk_details.risk_summary: The revision broadens software scope, increasing the risk of inadequate oversight.
risk_details.source_logic: Control enhancement explicitly addresses all organization-defined software, raising oversight complexity.
risk_details.evidence_source: Policy documentation and control test results.

compliance_records.PolicyTitle: NIST SP 800-53 Release 5.2.0 Security and Privacy Controls Amendment

ai_analysis.extraction_rationale: The amendment section lists all new, revised, and enhanced controls in NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5.2.0, providing the basis for extracting subpolicies.
